text:
Market today: The market's a bit tense, with U.S. Treasury yields ticking up and inflation broadening out. This isn't great for your tech/chip heavy book, as higher rates tend to weigh on growth stocks. Middle East concerns are still in play, though oil gains eased.

Your holdings: QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and QQQI are all in the red and flagged "SELL." SOXL and SPAL are particularly deep in drawdown (-46.77% and -47.83% respectively), and being leveraged, these drops are amplified. Remember, margin makes these drawdowns sting extra hard.

Watch: Keep an eye on the broader chip sector. While some strategists are divided on buying, Nvidia got a positive mention today. Your scanner also flags AMD and NVDA for potential long setups.

Bottom line: Focus on managing risk in your leveraged and underperforming positions, especially with rising yields and inflation headwinds.
plays: SOXL & SPAL: Your stops for these leveraged ETFs (SOXL stop 200, price 135.47; SPAL stop 21.38, price 13.12) are above current prices. This indicates a significant issue; re-evaluate these positions and their risk management immediately.
QQQ, SMH, QQQI: These are all marked "SELL" and showing losses. Monitor closely and be ready to act if they approach their stops at 670, 540, and 52 respectively.
No new buys today - hold and watch.
